title: Week 5 Case Study Predicting Employee Salary INFS3822
body:
INFS3822 – AI for Business Analytics



Week 5 Case Study Predicting Employee Salary

Background
ABC Corporation, a mid-sized technology company is facing significant challenges with high employee
turnover and struggles to offer competitive salaries to retain talented employees.
ABC aims to leverage AI-driven Business Analytics to accurately predict employee salaries, to address
the problem of employee turnover and make informed decisions about salary structures. This includes
setting competitive salaries, optimising their compensation strategy to align with industry standards and
employee expectations. This would also help HR managers gain valuable insights for long-term career
planning and development, including talent acquisition, employee retention, and overall workforce
planning.
By leveraging robust machine learning models using SAS Viya to predict employee salaries, ABC
company addresses critical business challenges related to employee retention, talent acquisition,
efficient budget allocation for salaries, and equitable pay across different roles. This empowers the
company to make informed business decisions, ensuring competitive and equitable salary structures
that support business decisions and employee satisfaction in a competitive job market.



Dataset
You are provided with a dataset of salaries of jobs in the Data Science domain. The dataset is available
on Moodle as an Excel spreadsheet called ds_salaries.xlsx under the Week 5 section. Source of the
dataset is https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/ruchi798/data-science-job-salaries


The dataset includes the following variables:
     •   work_year: The year the salary was paid.
     •   experience_level: The experience level of the employee with the following possible values:
            • EN for Entry-level / Junior experience
            • MI for Mid-level / Intermediate experience
            • SE for Senior-level / Expert experience
            • EX for Executive-level / Director experience
     •   employment_type: The type of employment for the role with the following possible values:
            • PT for Part-Time job
            • FT for Full-Time job
            • CT for Contract job
            • FL for Freelance job
     •   job_title: The role worked in during the year.
                                                                                                            CRICOS Provider Code 00098G




     •   salary: The total gross salary amount paid.
     •   salary_currency: The currency of the salary paid as an ISO 4217 currency code.



Page 1
      •   salary_in_usd: The salary in USD (FX rate divided by avg. USD rate for the respective year via
         fxdata.foorilla.com).
     •   employee_residence: Employee's primary country of residence in during the work year as an
         ISO 3166 country code.
     •   remote_ratio: The overall amount of work done remotely with the following possible values:
             • 0 for no remote work (less than 20%)
             • 50 for partially remote work (around 50%)
             • 100 for fully remote work (more than 80%)
     •   company_location: The country of the employer's main office or contracting branch as an ISO
         3166 country code.
     •   company_size: The average number of people that worked for the company during the year with
         the following possible values:
             • “S” for less than 50 employees (small)
             • “M” for 50 to 250 employees (medium)
             • “L” for more than 250 employees (large)



Hands-On Activity
Please read carefully the case study above and follow in-class instructions to build predictive models
using SAS Viya.
Discuss the findings of the predictive models and derive insights on how to address the business problem
of employee’s turnover.
Discuss the potential challenges you might encounter during the data analysis.




Page 2
